channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Biggest Google Pixel 9a leak so far reveals colors and seems to confirm the death of the camera bar
item.description: Not keen on the rumored redesign for the camera of the Pixel 9a? Bad news: another leak appears to confirm the bar is being ditched.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cisco Expands Certification and Training Courses for AI
item.description: Cisco announces new certification and training courses to help professionals develop artificial intelligence systems, focusing on skills-based AI readiness in three ways: introduction to modern AI, AI skills in professional roles, and updating existing learning materials.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Phones May Feature Silicon Carbon Batteries with Enhanced Capacity
item.description: Samsung's next-generation Galaxy phones might come equipped with silicon-carbon batteries, potentially offering capacities up to 7,000mAh.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Workday aims to manage AI agents like employees
item.description: California-based software provider Workday announced today the Workday Agent System of Record as part of its Workday Illuminate strategy to help organizations manage their AI agents.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft gets rid of ‘Edge uninstall’ advice page after facing criticism over it having nothing to do with removing the app, and just promoting the browser instead
item.description: Microsoft has made good headway with improving Edge in recent times, but it needs to stop with the promotional activity, particularly the weirder stuff.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Top translation apps for travelers
item.description: Travel abroad worry-free with these top translation apps for travelers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson highlights some favorites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The future of indoor air quality: TCL's FreshIN 3.0 brings energy-saving innovation home
item.description: No matter what you’re doing this festive season, from binging the best movies and shows to sharing special memories with your loved ones, TCL has a high-tech solution for you.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The lazy CIO’s guide to better IT investment
item.description: If you’re like most CIOs, you’re under pressure to introduce exciting new technological capabilities to the enterprise at large, promoting visions of digital transformation, generative AI, AGI, AI transformation, agentic AI, or some other version of being an FBC CIO — that’s “Fully Buzzword Complian
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk’s $97B offer to buy OpenAI rejected as leadership stands firm
item.description: Elon Musk and a consortium of investors made a $97.4 billion offer to acquire the nonprofit controlling OpenAI, escalating a battle over the future of the ChatGPT maker, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: VPN vs. ZTNA: Cisco tackles pros and cons
item.description: It’s no secret that more modern approaches to remote access have been usurping VPNs as organizations adapt to the realities of a distributed workforce, cloud-based applications, and heightened security threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sam Altman says 'no thank you' to Musk-led group's bid for control of OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO and co-founder Sam Altman appeared to shut down a reported offer from an investor group led by Elon Musk to buy the nonprofit that controls OpenAI, marking the latest back-and-forth between the two tech leaders.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NetOps teams ready to dump incumbent monitoring tools
item.description: Replacing a network management tool is not a trivial exercise, especially in large and complex networks. It can take months to install and tune a tool so that it is delivering value.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame scores big with Wi-Fi 6E stadium upgrade
item.description: When 80,000 fans fill the University of Notre Dame’s iconic football stadium on Saturday afternoons in the fall to cheer on the Fighting Irish, there’s no shortage of high-decibel screaming. And now, thanks to a major network expansion, there’s no shortage of high-bandwidth streaming.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok's US operations
item.description: Speaking virtually at a conference in Germany last month, Musk said he didn't envisage purchasing the social media app.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Spy vs spy: Security agencies help secure the network edge
item.description: The national intelligence services of five countries have offered enterprises advice on beating spies at their own game in a series of documents intended to help them protect network edge devices and appliances such as firewalls, routers, VPN (virtual private networks) gateways, internet of things (
item.dc:creator: Network World
